title: Tyson Foods: A Cyclical Turnaround Play
text: One of the industries hardest hit by the inflationary environment has been food production. Most food producers are suffering higher input costs such as livestock, grain and feed as well as labor and supply chain issues.

title: Tyson Foods (TSN) Fuels Growth With Williams Sausage Buyout
text: Tyson Foods' (TSN) acquisition of Williams Sausage Company is likely to solidify the former's product portfolio and manufacturing capacity.

title: Tyson Foods Completes Acquisition of Williams Sausage Company
text: SPRINGDALE, Ark., May 22, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Today, Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE: TSN) completed the previously announced acquisition of Williams Sausage Company, Inc. of Union City, Tenn., furthering the company's strategy to increase its capacity and product portfolio. To ensure the continuity of business operations, Emily Billingsley, Roger Williams' daughter, will be responsible for the company's operations.

title: 3 Meat Food Stocks to Watch as Industry Prospects Look Bright

title: 3 Stocks That Activist Investors Need to Target Next

title: Tyson Foods: Deep Drop After Earnings Presents Opportunity
text: Tyson Foods fell hard after their earnings, but things have been stabilizing a bit since. I believe that shares were cheap previously, and despite a loss, the shares still look cheap.

title: Tyson's Terrible Quarter Raises 2 Big Questions for Investors
text: Weak gross margins point to a mismatch between the company's costs and what it's charging customers. While Tyson's cost of goods sold jumped 11% year over year in its fiscal Q2, revenue barely budged.

title: Tyson Foods stock on track for worst weekly performance since November of 2008
text: Tyson Foods Inc.'s stock TSN, -0.92% is on track for a 20% decline on the week, which would be its worst performance since November of 2008, at the height of the financial crisis, according to Dow Jones Market Data. The stock has fallen after meat producer swung to a surprise second-quarter loss and lowered its sales guidance, hurt by charges stemming from plant closures and job cuts, as well as weak demand for meat.

title: Tyson Foods To Participate in 18th Annual BMO Global Farm to Market Conference

title: Tyson Foods stock slides after meat producer swings to surprise loss
text: Tyson Foods Inc. stock fell 16% Monday after the meat producer swung to a surprise second-quarter loss and lowered its sales guidance, hurt by charges stemming from plant closures and job cuts, as well as weak demand for meat.

title: Tyson Foods (TSN) Shares Slide on Q2 Loss, Slashed Sales View
text: Tyson Foods' (TSN) fiscal second-quarter results reflect a tough protein market. Management cuts its fiscal 2023 revenue forecast, though it is ahead of its plan with regard to its productivity program.

title: Tyson Foods (TSN) Reports Q2 Loss, Lags Revenue Estimates
text: Tyson Foods (TSN) came out with a quarterly loss of $0.04 per share versus the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.81. This compares to earnings of $2.29 per share a year ago.

title: Tyson Foods stock slumps as investors digest 2Q loss
text: Tyson Foods (NYSE:TSN) stock sank over 11% in pre-market Monday trading after it reported an unexpected second-quarter loss due to rising costs and weaker prices and cut its full-year sales guidance.  Arkansas-based Tyson, one of the world's largest food companies, reported flat sales of $13.1 billion for the three months to March 31, 2023, below the consensus estimate of $13.6 billion.

title: Tyson Foods: Is It Time To Cut Losses Or Load Up On Shares?
text: Current holders of Tyson Foods NYSE: TSN may be trimming their positions, but those looking to build a position in a healthy consumer staples stock with a sector-beating yield might want to put it on their watchlists. Headwinds within the meat industry cut into the Q2 results and led to reduced guidance that has shares moving lower, but there is only so far the stock can fall.
